title: Migrate to Grafana Cloud — Self-managed steps

blocks.content: This guide walks you through the steps performed on your self-managed Grafana instance: connecting to Grafana Cloud, building a migration snapshot, and uploading your resources.
You'll confirm your environment meets the requirements for a successful migration.
Verify you have Grafana server administrator access on this self-managed instance and Admin role access on your target Grafana Cloud stack.
Confirm this instance has internet access. If you use a highly available setup, scale down to a single replica before migrating.

If your network uses an allowlist, add the required Grafana Cloud IPs and URLs. Refer to the [Grafana Cloud Migration Assistant documentation](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/administration/migrate-to-grafana-cloud/) for the current list.
You confirmed your environment is ready for migration.
You'll connect this self-managed instance to your Grafana Cloud stack using the migration token generated in the Cloud guide.
Open the navigation menu and select **Administration** > **General** > **Migrate to Grafana Cloud**.
Click **Migrate this instance to Cloud**.
Paste the migration token from your Cloud instance into the **Migration token** field and click **Connect to this Stack**.
You connected your self-managed instance to Grafana Cloud and can now build a migration snapshot.
You'll select which resources to migrate and create a snapshot of their current state.
Review the available resource types: dashboards, folders, data sources, app plugins, panel plugins, library panels, and alerting resources.
Select the checkbox next to each resource type you want to migrate. Dependencies are automatically included when you select a resource that requires them.
Click **Build snapshot**.
Wait for the snapshot to complete. The interface displays progress as resources are captured.
You created a snapshot of your selected resources, ready for upload to Grafana Cloud.
You'll upload the snapshot to transfer your resources to Grafana Cloud with real-time progress tracking.
Click **Upload snapshot** to begin the transfer.
Monitor the real-time progress as the **Status** column updates for each resource. Successfully migrated resources show **Uploaded to cloud**.
For any resources with errors, expand the error details to understand what requires manual resolution.
You uploaded your resources to Grafana Cloud and can now verify them in your Cloud instance using the Cloud guide.
You'll optionally re-migrate alert rules in their original active state instead of manually resuming them in Cloud.

Edit your Grafana configuration file (`grafana.ini` or `custom.ini`) and add:

```ini
[cloud_migration]
alert_rules_state = unchanged
```
Save the configuration file and restart your Grafana instance.
Navigate back to **Administration** > **General** > **Migrate to Grafana Cloud**.
Click **Reconfigure snapshot** to return to the resource selection screen.
Select your resources and click **Build snapshot** to create a new snapshot with the updated alert rule state.
Click **Upload snapshot** to re-upload your resources. Alert rules are migrated in their original state without creating duplicates.
You re-migrated alert rules in their original active state, avoiding the need to manually resume them in Cloud.
You completed the self-managed migration steps. Switch to the Cloud guide to verify your resources and activate alert rules in Grafana Cloud.
